confirmations.reason: Workspace Rules are stored in .agents/rules at the workspace or git root, directly serving as a project-scoped instruction file that gives the agent standing instructions and context for that repo/workspace.

Plugins are namespaced bundles grouping skills, rules, MCP servers, and hooks into a single package — directly matching the capability of bundling reusable capabilities/instructions the agent can load.

The Lifecycle Hooks feature explicitly runs custom local shell scripts or commands at specific lifecycle points during execution, directly matching the capability of running custom logic on agent events.

Rules provide persistent, reusable constraints/context but are not specifically about shaping system prompts, persona, or output style. They are general behavioral constraints, not output/system-prompt customization.
The Reasoning Model feature explicitly lets users choose from multiple frontier reasoning models to power the agent, directly realizing model selection.
MCP Integration directly implements the Model Context Protocol as a standard way to connect external tools, data, and services to the agent.
The built-in MCP server manager directly addresses installing, configuring, and managing MCP servers, matching the capability of MCP server management and registry.
The feature explicitly describes OAuth authentication for MCP servers with browser/console authorization flows, directly realizing MCP authentication.
Per-Tool MCP Permission grants access to a single named tool on a specific MCP server, directly realizing fine-grained allow-listing/governance of which MCP tools the agent may use.
The Memory feature is explicitly a searchable, cross-session knowledge store agents can query to recall information beyond the current context, directly realizing persistent agent memory.
The Subagents feature explicitly describes the primary agent delegating long-running operations to parallel autonomous subagents, directly realizing the spawn-and-delegate subagent capability.
Multi-Agent Orchestration describes coordinating multiple agents with error recovery, retries, and coordination logic — directly realizing multi-agent teams and messaging/coordination.

Workflows are saved markdown files defining repeatable series of steps to guide the agent through tasks across sessions, directly matching the capability of defining repeatable named multi-step task sequences.
Multi-turn plan generation mode explicitly supports a planning mode that generates multi-turn plans for complex tasks before execution, directly realizing plan-then-execute mode.

Verification loops describe running test/build commands and iterating after changes, but this is reactive test-driven iteration, not setting a verifiable 'done' condition and having the agent autonomously loop until that goal is met. It lacks the goal-directed autonomous loop framing.
Worktree Support explicitly has agents operating in dedicated Git worktrees to isolate parallel work within a project, directly realizing parallel isolated workspaces.
Agent Runtime is described as a Google Cloud-hosted, fully managed auto-scaling runtime that executes deployed agents in the cloud, directly realizing the cloud execution environment capability.
Background Tasks explicitly run tracked, long-running operations asynchronously without blocking the primary agent, directly realizing background/async execution.
Scheduled Tasks let users schedule messages to agents with repeatable time-based triggers, directly realizing scheduled/triggered runs without supervision.
Terminal Sandboxing provides kernel-level/native OS isolation for terminal commands executed by the agent, directly realizing sandboxed execution to limit blast radius.
The Fine-Grained Permissions Engine represents every sensitive agent operation as a permission in a unified engine, directly realizing tool permission controls to approve/deny agent actions.
Independent Network Access Control explicitly controls network connectivity for sandboxed commands independently of filesystem restrictions, directly realizing network/egress control.
Agent activity logging provides structured, configurable logging of agent behavior with OpenTelemetry support, directly realizing audit logging and telemetry for review/compliance.
Quota Tracking lets users monitor their active AI credit quota and consumption directly in the CLI, directly realizing cost and usage tracking.
Resuming sessions explicitly allows users to return to prior conversations to continue an implementation or refine an existing solution, directly realizing resume/fork session capability.

Projects define folder/repository boundaries and scope for agents, but they are a workspace/project organizational construct, not an access surface (i.e., not about where the agent can be used: terminal, IDE, web, cloud, mobile). This is a project-scoping feature, not an access-surface enumeration.
